agent.runner_summary: Runs the SkyServe airline support agent through the simulator adapter with plain string chat turns passed directly as user messages.

components.when_to_use: Use for booking lookup failures or when validating confirmation-code handling in isolation.
components.input_guidance: Use named JSON arguments matching the tool schema, for example {"confirmation_code": "SKY123"}. The demo data includes SKY123 and BAY777.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned booking summary string, including passenger, route, departure, seat, and status details.

components.when_to_use: Use for baggage-policy answers or ticket-type normalization behavior.
components.input_guidance: Use named JSON arguments matching the tool schema, for example {"ticket_type": "basic economy"}.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned policy text for the requested ticket type.

components.when_to_use: Use for seat-change behavior, especially confirmation-code validation and seat formatting.
components.input_guidance: Use named JSON arguments matching the tool schema, for example {"confirmation_code": "SKY123", "requested_seat": "14C"}.
components.output_guidance: Evaluate the returned confirmation or validation error string, and mock the tool when the scenario should avoid mutating shared in-memory booking state.

log_notes: Agent learning environments should pass a plain string as FixedTurn.content; the simulator adapter treats it as the exact user chat message.
Mock-call transcript entries for airline_support.agent:change_seat are useful when testing seat-change flows without mutating the in-memory demo bookings.
learning_env_guidance: Use plain string turns that match a single user chat message exactly; do not wrap the message in JSON.
Representative agent turns can reference the demo confirmation codes SKY123 or BAY777 when you want the agent to exercise booking lookup or seat-change behavior.
Prefer the default OpenAI provider path used by the OpenAI Agents SDK and ensure OPENAI_API_KEY is available at runtime.
Choose component environments for the three direct function tools when the behavior under test is narrower than the full support conversation.
